back_translation: Give ear, O heavens, and I will speak, and let the earth hear the words of my mouth.
My teaching shall drop like the rain; my speech shall distill like the dew, like gentle showers on the grass, and like showers on the herb.
For the name of the Lord I will proclaim; give greatness to our God.
The Rock, perfect is His work, for all His ways are justice; a God of faithfulness and without iniquity, righteous and upright is He.
They have acted corruptly toward Him; not His children, their blemish; a perverse and crooked generation.
Is this how you repay the Lord, O foolish and not wise people? Is He not your Father, who created you? He made you and established you.
Remember the days of old; consider the years of generation and generation. Ask your father, and he will tell you, your elders, and they will say to you.
When the Most High gave inheritance to nations, when He divided the sons of man, He set the borders of the peoples according to the number of the sons of Israel.
For the portion of the Lord is His people; Jacob is the cord of His inheritance.
He found him in a desert land, and in the wasteland, a howling of wilderness; He surrounded him, He instructed him, He guarded him as the pupil of His eye.
Like an eagle stirring its nest, over its young hovering, He spread His wings, took him, carried him on His pinion.
The Lord alone led him, and there was not with him a foreign god.
He made him ride on the heights of the land, and he ate the produce of the field; and He suckled him honey from the rock, and oil from the flinty rock.
Curds of the herd, and milk of the flock, with fat of lambs, and rams, sons of Bashan, and male goats, with fat of the kidneys of wheat; and the blood of the grape you drank, fermented wine.
And Jeshurun grew fat and kicked—you grew fat, you grew thick, you became stuffed—and he abandoned the God who made him, and dishonored the Rock of his salvation.
They provoked Him to jealousy with foreigners; with abominations they angered Him.
They sacrificed to demons, not God, to gods they did not know, new ones from nearby that came, your fathers did not fear them.
The Rock who gave you birth, you neglected, and you forgot the God who labored to bring you forth.
And the Lord saw, and He rejected, from the anger of His sons and His daughters.
And He said: I will hide My face from them; I will see what is their end, for a generation of perversions are they, sons in whom is no faithfulness.
They provoked Me to jealousy with what is not God; they angered Me with their worthless idols; and I, I will provoke them with what is not a people; with a foolish nation I will anger them.
For a fire is kindled in My anger, and it burns to the lower Sheol; and it consumes the earth and its produce, and it sets the foundations of the mountains aflame.
I will heap upon them evils; My arrows I will spend on them.
Wasted with hunger, and devoured by pestilence and bitter plague; and the fang of beasts I will send against them, with the venom of those crawling in the dust.
From outside the sword shall bereave, and from the inner rooms terror—both young man and young woman, suckling with the gray-haired man.
I said: I will scatter them; I will cause to cease from man their memory.
Were it not that I dreaded the anger of the enemy, lest their adversaries should misunderstand, lest they should say: Our hand is high, and not the Lord has done all this.
For a nation devoid of counsel are they, and there is no understanding in them.
If only they were wise, they would understand this; they would consider their end.
How could one pursue a thousand, and two put ten thousand to flight, if not that their Rock had sold them, and the Lord had given them up?
For not like our Rock is their rock, even our enemies being judges.
For from the vine of Sodom is their vine, and from the fields of Gomorrah; their grapes are grapes of poison, clusters of bitterness to them.
The venom of serpents is their wine, and the head-poison of cobras, cruel.
Is it not stored up with Me, sealed in My treasuries?
Mine is vengeance and repayment, at the time their foot shall slip; for the day of their calamity is near, and prepared things are hastening for them.
For the Lord will judge His people, and on His servants He will have compassion, when He sees that the hand is gone, and none is left, slave or free.
And He will say: Where are their gods, the rock in whom they took refuge?
Who ate the fat of their sacrifices, who drank the wine of their drink offerings? Let them rise up and help you; let there be over you a shelter!
See now that I, I am He, and there is no God beside Me; I, I kill, and I make alive; I have wounded, and I, I will heal, and there is none who delivers from My hand.
For I lift My hand to the heavens, and I will say: As I live forever,
if I sharpen the flash of My sword, and My hand grasps in judgment, I will return vengeance to My adversaries, and to those who hate Me I will repay.
I will make My arrows drunk from blood, and My sword shall eat flesh; from the blood of the slain and the captive, from the head of the leaders of the enemy.
Rejoice, O nations, with His people, for the blood of His servants He will avenge, and vengeance He will return to His adversaries, and He will atone for His ground, His people.
And Moses came and spoke all the words of this song in the ears of the people, he and Hoshea son of Nun.
And Moses finished speaking all these words to all Israel.
And he said to them: Set your hearts on all the words that I am testifying against you today, which you shall command your sons to keep to do all the words of this law.
For it is not an empty word for you, for it is your life, and by this word you shall prolong days upon the ground where you are crossing the Jordan there to possess it.
And the Lord spoke to Moses on the very same day, saying:
Go up to this Mount of the Abarim, Mount Nebo, which is in the land of Moab, which is opposite Jericho, and see the land of Canaan, which I am giving to the sons of Israel as a possession.
And die on the mountain where you are going up there, and be gathered to your people, just as Aaron your brother died on Mount Hor and was gathered to his people.
Because you broke faith with Me in the midst of the sons of Israel at the waters of Meribah-kadesh in the wilderness of Zin, because you did not sanctify Me in the midst of the sons of Israel.
For from opposite you shall see the land, but there you shall not enter, to the land that I am giving to the sons of Israel.
